title: Audit a test suite — which modules can reclaim async: true
prompt:
Given this inventory of test modules and their justifications, write `docs/async_audit.md` listing which modules can be safely changed to `async: true` and which must stay `async: false`:

1. `AccountsTest` — async: false, uses `create_user/1`
2. `RateLimiterTest` — async: false, writes to `Application.put_env(:my_app, :rate_limit, ...)`
3. `ReportsTest` — async: false, spawns a Task via Task.Supervisor
4. `FullStackIntegrationTest` — async: false, uses `mode(:shared)` to cover a Registry
5. `AccountsContextTest` — async: false, reads from Repo only

Must state: (1) can be async, (2) must stay false (global state), (3) can be async via allow/3, (4) must stay false (shared mode), (5) can be async.
tier_rationale: 5-item classification with different criteria per item. Requires applying the full rule set.
